controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Configurable controller that supports Endpoint, Root Port, Dual‑Mode, and switch port personalities. Device type can be selected via parameters or pins at build/integration time. Enables embedded host controller operation as RP/DM for system root complex use cases. Exposes full PCIe configuration space and capability chains per selected role. Supports standard enumeration and discovery flows.
evidence: Parameter Descriptions: Device Type (Main Features Config Parameters), p. 11; Register Descriptions intro, p. 3030
dependencies: Device type parameter/pin strap; proper reset sequencing
Remarks: Select RP/DM for host role; EP for device role

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Implements the standard PIPE interface to connect to external PCIe PHYs. Includes a PHY register bus for control/status and calibration. Supports custom PHY option when integrating vendor‑specific PHYs. Ensures compliance with PCIe physical layer timing and signaling. Provides lane/clock interfaces per configured link width and speed.
evidence: Parameter Descriptions: Phy Selection, p. 17; Signal Descriptions: PHY Register Bus Interface Signals, p. 1198
dependencies: External PHY availability; PIPE clocks/resets; board SI
Remarks: Choose PHY IP that matches target Gen/lane width

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Provides AXI interfaces on the system side for configuration and high‑bandwidth data movement. Supports basic and advanced AXI configuration options including outstanding IDs and QoS. Bridges PCIe transactions to system memory coherently when used with system fabric. Enables mapping of BARs and DMA buffers into system address space. Includes robust backpressure and flow control on AXI.
evidence: Parameter Descriptions: Basic AXI Config, p. 42; Advanced AXI Config, p. 77
dependencies: AXI clock/reset; fabric connectivity and address map
Remarks: Size AXI data width/IDs to match throughput goals

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Integrated DMA engines provide read and write paths with multiple channels. Per‑channel enable, context, and MSI configuration registers exist for fine‑grained control. Hardware doorbells and status support efficient submission/completion. Designed for high throughput with minimal CPU overhead. Exposes capability via a Vendor‑Specific Extended Capability structure.
evidence: DMA_READ_ENGINE_CHGROUP_OFF (USP) p. 5142; HDMA_MSI_MSGD_OFF_WRCH_i/HDMA_EN_OFF_RDCH_i (DSP) p. 2970–2971; VSECDMA_EXT_CAP_HDR_OFF p. 3837
dependencies: CC_DMA_ENABLE; Bus Master Enable in PCI Command; MSI/MSI‑X configured
Remarks: Use unrolled register map to program per‑channel contexts

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Dedicated context and read buffer RAMs store descriptors and data staging. Handshake and context memories decouple software and hardware rates. Buffering improves large transfer efficiency and latency hiding. Internal RAMs are sized per configuration to meet bandwidth targets. Exposed through DMA programming model and signals.
evidence: Signal Descriptions: DMA Context Memory RAM, p. 894; DMA Read Buffer RAM, p. 898; DMA Handshake Signals, p. 896
dependencies: DMA base/unroll programmed; memory regions accessible; link up
Remarks: Pin descriptor rings in contiguous memory for best performance

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Hardware handshake counters and doorbells coordinate work submission and completion. Low/high handshake counter registers track outstanding descriptors. Reduces interrupt load by batching completions. Supports polling or MSI/MSI‑X notification models. Aids robust flow control in high‑throughput scenarios.
evidence: DMA_WRITE_ENGINE_HSHAKE_CNT_LOW/HIGH_OFF (USP) p. 5144/5147; Handshake Signals p. 896
dependencies: DMA enabled; MSI/MSI‑X configured if interrupt‑driven
Remarks: Use polling for microbenchmarks; MSI‑X for production

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Internal retry and start‑of‑transfer buffers improve link utilization and reliability. Retransmission is handled in hardware on NAKs or errors. SOT buffering helps align TLP boundaries to PHY framing. These buffers are dimensioned for negotiated Gen and width. Tuning equalization works in concert with these buffers for stability.
evidence: Signal Descriptions: Transmit Retry Buffer RAM, p. 882; SOT Buffer RAM, p. 887
dependencies: Link trained; PHY stable; adequate credit flow
Remarks: Purely internal; no driver programming beyond link bring‑up

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Implements standard MSI capability for interrupt delivery without legacy pins. Supports 32/64‑bit message addresses and multiple MSI vectors when enabled. PF and VF contexts expose MSI enable/mask/address/data. Handshake signals integrate with internal event sources and DMA. Reduces latency and improves scalability compared to INTx.
evidence: PF0 MSI Capability regs: PCI_MSI_CAP_ID_NEXT_CTRL_REG p. 1357; MSI address/data regs p. 1361+; Signal Descriptions: MSI Interface Signals (2.91)
dependencies: MSI_CAP_ENABLE && (CX_MEMORY_MAP_PCIE_TYPE<2); Bus Master Enable; configured address/data
Remarks: Program MSI before arming producers to avoid lost interrupts

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Supports MSI‑X with a programmable table and Pending Bit Array for fine‑grained vectoring. Enables per‑vector masking and up to thousands of vectors per function. Table and PBA can reside in BAR‑mapped memory for flexible placement. Internal RAM interfaces back MSI‑X accesses. Ideal for multi‑queue drivers and virtualization.
evidence: PF0 MSI‑X regs: PCI_MSIX_CAP_ID_NEXT_CTRL p. 1469; MSIX_TABLE_OFFSET p. 1472; MSIX_PBA_OFFSET p. 1481; MSI‑X Table/PBA RAM Signals p. 890/892
dependencies: MSIX_CAP_ENABLE && (CX_MEMORY_MAP_PCIE_TYPE<2); Table/PBA BAR mapped; Function Mask handling
Remarks: Do not modify entries while MSI‑X Enable=0 to prevent spurious IRQs

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Provides compatibility with legacy level‑triggered interrupts. Interrupt Line and Pin fields are exposed in configuration headers. Useful for simple firmware bring‑up or OSes lacking MSI support. Typically disabled in production in favor of MSI/MSI‑X. Supports standard INTA#..INTD# semantics.
evidence: Type0/Type1 headers: MAX_LATENCY_MIN_GRANT_INTERRUPT_PIN_INTERRUPT_LINE_REG p. 3114; BRIDGE_CTRL_INT_PIN_INT_LINE_REG p. 1342
dependencies: INTx unmasked; platform interrupt controller wired
Remarks: Prefer MSI/MSI‑X for performance and scalability

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Virtual Functions inherit MSI/MSI‑X capabilities with per‑VF enable/mask/address. Enables scalable interrupt delivery in virtualized environments. VF configuration space provides capability pointers and headers. Works with PF‑managed MSI‑X tables for resource partitioning. Integrates with hypervisors and SR‑IOV‑aware drivers.
evidence: Signal Descriptions: VF MSI/MSI‑X signals; VF_PCI_CAP_PTR_REG (USP) p. 4726
dependencies: SR‑IOV enabled; VF BARs mapped; hypervisor support
Remarks: Coordinate vector allocation between PF and VFs

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Implements standard PCI PM capability for D0–D3hot and PME signaling. Controls device power states and reports PME status to upstream entities. Integrates with OS power frameworks. Coexists with deeper PCIe link‑level power mechanisms. Supports wake events per capability bits.
evidence: PF PM Capability in PCI Cap chain (PF/VF); standard PMCAP offsets (PF around PCI_CAP chain, manual p. 1350s)
dependencies: OS PM stack; PME routing configured
Remarks: Use alongside L1 substates for optimal idle power

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Supports PCIe L1 Substates to reduce idle link power with acceptable resume latency. Includes configurable defaults for latency tolerance and common mode restore. Works with endpoint acceptable latency parameters. Requires end‑to‑end support across link partners. Provides significant power savings in idle periods.
evidence: Parameter Descriptions: L1 Substates/L1.CPM Capability p. 122–125; Endpoint L1 Acceptable Latency table
dependencies: Both link partners enable L1.1/L1.2; latency budgets validated
Remarks: Tune buffering and wake times before enabling globally

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
feature_name: Link Power Control via PCIe Capability
description: Standard Device and Link Control registers manage ASPM, Max Payload Size, and Max Read Request Size. Software can enable ASPM policy and adjust payload for performance. Link status reflects negotiated speed and width for runtime decisions. Slot control/status supported where applicable. Provides knobs for balancing power and throughput.
evidence: DEVICE_CAPABILITIES p. 1373; DEVICE_CONTROL/STATUS p. 1377; LINK_CAPABILITIES p. 1388; LINK_CONTROL/STATUS p. 1395
dependencies: Link trained; OS policy allows ASPM/MPS/MRRS changes
Remarks: Increase MPS/MRRS after validation to boost throughput

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Implements PCI‑SIG Power Budgeting Extended Capability for reporting and control. Allows software to read and program budgeting data structures. Useful in multi‑function and chassis‑managed systems. Exposes data select and capability registers for structured access. Integrates into platform power orchestration.
evidence: PB_BASE/SELECT/DATA/CAP regs (DSP) p. 1579–1585
dependencies: Capability present; management stack uses it
Remarks: Optional but valuable for system‑level power reporting

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Provides register control to gate internal clocks during idle periods. Reduces dynamic power when traffic is quiescent. Should be coordinated with link and DMA idleness to avoid throughput impact. Can be managed dynamically by firmware. Complements ASPM/L1 Substates for holistic power savings.
evidence: CLOCK_GATING_CTRL_OFF +0x88C (USP) p. 2245
dependencies: Traffic quiescent; driver/firmware policy
Remarks: Gate progressively and monitor latency effects

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Adds comprehensive error logging, severity control, and reporting beyond baseline PCIe. Captures correctable, non‑fatal, and fatal error sources with TLP headers. Supports error masking and forwarding to root complex. Essential for robust RAS and field diagnostics. Integrates with OS AER frameworks for automated handling.
evidence: PF0_AER_CAP: AER_EXT_CAP_HDR_OFF (DSP) p. 1488; Exists: AER_ENABLE && (CX_MEMORY_MAP_PCIE_TYPE<2)
dependencies: AER_ENABLE; OS AER service active
Remarks: Unmask only vetted errors to avoid spurious resets

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Per‑lane error status registers expose physical layer error conditions. Aids in detecting marginal channels and SI issues. Useful during validation and field diagnostics. Complements AER by providing lane‑granular visibility. Can trigger re‑training or maintenance actions by software.
evidence: LANE_ERR_STATUS_REG (DSP) p. 1591; (USP) p. 3362
dependencies: Secondary PCIe Capability present (Gen3+)
Remarks: Poll during stress tests; log alongside AER events

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Exposes margining controls and per‑lane status to assess eye openings and link quality. Supports software‑driven margin sweeps. Helps correlate BER and equalization settings. Valuable for platform bring‑up and RMA analysis. Works with higher‑gen PHYs supporting margining.
evidence: MARGIN_PORT_CAPABILITIES_STATUS p. 3392; MARGIN_LANE_CNTRL_STATUSi p. 3394
dependencies: Margining support in PHY/PL32G/PL64G blocks
Remarks: Use offline or maintenance windows to avoid performance impact

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Controller fabric exposes consolidated internal error status. Helps detect AXI/fabric protocol violations and timeouts. Enables quicker triage of system‑side failures. Works alongside PCIe‑visible AER for full coverage. Status bits can be tied to interrupts or polled.
evidence: CXS_ERROR_STAT_OFF (USP) p. 3834
dependencies: Controller integrated fabric present; error interrupt wired
Remarks: Clear after logging to avoid missed subsequent events

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Build‑time options allow enabling ECC or parity on internal RAMs. Improves reliability against soft errors in context and buffer memories. Error flags integrate with controller status for reporting. May include correction and scrub policies depending on configuration. Recommended for safety‑critical designs.
evidence: Parameter Descriptions: Advanced RAM Config, p. 196; RAM signal sections (Retry/DMA RAMs)
dependencies: Feature availability depends on configured core options
Remarks: Consult synthesis config for exact ECC/parity coverage

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Implements ACS to constrain peer‑to‑peer and control request forwarding. Provides egress control vectors and upstream forwarding control. Enhances isolation for virtualization and multi‑tenant systems. Exposes per‑function control and status. Works with IOMMU to enforce DMA isolation.
evidence: ACS Register Configuration (PF0) p. 147; Signals: cfg_acs_* and VF equivalents
dependencies: Upstream fabric honors ACS; enable in config space
Remarks: Enable on RPs and DPs to prevent unintended P2P

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Allows functions to cache IOMMU translations and request address translations on demand. Reduces IOTLB miss penalties in virtualized and shared memory systems. Requires end‑to‑end support including IOMMU and OS. Capability controls are exposed in PF configuration space. Works with PASID and optionally PRI for fine‑grained control.
evidence: Signal Descriptions: ATS Capability Signals p. 1201; ATS Register Configuration (PF0) p. 149
dependencies: IOMMU present and configured; ACS/ARI settings compatible
Remarks: Enable only when system IOMMU supports ATS coherently

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Adds PASID capability and TLP prefix support to tag requests with process IDs. Enables per‑process isolation for DMA with shared devices. Works in concert with ATS/PRI and modern IOMMUs. Configurable defaults are provided in capability registers. Critical for SR‑IOV and SVA (Shared Virtual Addressing) deployments.
evidence: Parameter Descriptions: PASID Capability Register Defaults p. 129; TLP Prefix Support p. 127
dependencies: IOMMU with PASID enabled; software stack support
Remarks: Coordinate PASID width with OS/IOMMU limits

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Supports device‑initiated page requests to handle faults in shared virtual memory. Enables on‑demand mapping of pages by the IOMMU. Reduces stalls for working sets larger than pinned memory. Complements ATS and PASID for full SVA. Particularly useful in accelerator and GPU‑like workloads.
evidence: Parameter Descriptions: PRI Capability (not shown in excerpt; referenced in notes)
dependencies: IOMMU with PRI; end‑to‑end PRI enablement
Remarks: Verify OS PRI support before enabling in production

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Expands function number space and simplifies SR‑IOV hierarchies. Provides ARI capability and control registers. Enables better scaling for VFs behind a single PF. Must be consistently enabled across the path. Improves enumeration density without external bridges.
evidence: ARI Extended Capability: ARI_BASE/CAP_REG (USP) p. 3351–3353
dependencies: ARI enable in all upstream components
Remarks: Enable before SR‑IOV to maximize VF addressing space

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Implements Single Root I/O Virtualization with PF and multiple VFs. Provides VF BARs, capability pointers, and error reporting hooks. PF controls provisioning and isolation of VFs. Integrates with MSI/MSI‑X for per‑VF interrupt scaling. Essential for cloud and multi‑tenant systems.
evidence: SR‑IOV Register Configuration (PF0) p. 185; VF BARs p. 188; VF PCI config p. 4726; EXTSRIOV signals p. 1188/1194
dependencies: Platform SR‑IOV support; IOMMU; PF driver
Remarks: Plan BAR sizes carefully to fit VF windows

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Supports enclosure management functions via NPEM extended capability. Provides control and status registers for sensors and indicators. Enables chassis‑level monitoring and control through PCIe. Useful in servers and storage backplanes. Integrates into platform management stacks.
evidence: PF0_NPEM_CAP: NPEM_EXT_CAP_HDR/CTRL/STATUS (USP) p. 3847–3862; (DSP) p. 2014+
dependencies: NPEM_ENABLE; management software present
Remarks: Map BAR space exposing NPEM registers to BMC/host tools

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Adds Gen3+ controls like Link Control 3 and lane error reporting. Provides knobs for equalization and advanced link features. Exposes speed change and retraining policies. Improves interoperability at higher data rates. Standard extended capability recognized by OS.
evidence: PF0_SPCIE_CAP (DSP) p. 1587; LINK_CONTROL3 and lane controls p. 1587–1592
dependencies: Gen3+ negotiated or targeted; capability present
Remarks: OS enumerates; drivers may adjust for tuning only

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Extended capabilities expose controls/status for Gen4/Gen5/Gen6‑class PHY blocks. Software can read link status and limited controls for equalization and training. Supports margining and advanced diagnostics at higher rates. Backwards compatible with lower generations. Requires matching PHY silicon and board design.
evidence: PL64G_EXT_CAP_HDR/CAP/CTRL/STATUS (USP) p. 3422–3426; GEN3_RELATED and EQ regs p. 2247–2261
dependencies: CX_PLxxG_ENABLE; external PHY supports target Gen; SI budget
Remarks: Use only for supervised tuning; defaults are standards‑compliant

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Fine‑grained per‑lane equalization controls and FOM tables are provided. Enables manual override of presets and coefficients when needed. Useful for platform bring‑up and solving marginal channels. Can improve eye opening and BER with careful tuning. Changes should be validated across PVT corners.
evidence: GEN3_FCOEF_LANEx_y (USP) p. 4265–4273; PL64G lane EQ regs p. 3430+
dependencies: Gen>=3 link; access to EQ registers; trained link
Remarks: Record baseline settings before overrides for rollback

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
feature_name: Negotiated Lane Width and Bandwidth Scaling
description: Supports scalable lane widths with automatic negotiation at link training. Link status registers expose final speed and width for software policy. Bandwidth scales with both negotiated speed and lane count. Works with upstream/downstream partners to find best common mode. Enables power/perf trade‑offs via retraining if desired.
evidence: LINK_CAPABILITIES p. 1388; LINK_CONTROL/STATUS p. 1395
dependencies: Board routing and PHY determine max width; partner capabilities
Remarks: Use retrain to recover width after transient errors

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Exposes standard Type 0/Type 1 headers with programmable BARs and resources. Supports ROM BAR, subsystem IDs, capability pointer chains, and bridge controls. Enables memory and I/O resource mapping by system firmware/OS. Required for enumeration of PF/VF functions. Provides interrupt line/pin fields for legacy paths.
evidence: Type1 Header regs p. 1302–1342; PF0 BARs p. 3102–3114; PCI_CAP_PTR_REG p. 3113
dependencies: BAR apertures sized/mapped by firmware; Bus Master Enable
Remarks: Respect DBI write rules when changing sticky defaults

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Implements full PCIe capability and extended capability lists. Enables discovery of features such as AER, SR‑IOV, ARI, ATS, and vendor‑specific blocks. Software walks the linked list via capability pointers. Supports standards‑compliant layout for interoperability. Basis for feature negotiation and enablement.
evidence: PCI_CAP_PTR_REG (PF) p. 3113; multiple extended capability headers cited
dependencies: Correct capability pointer chain; DBI access when needed
Remarks: Probe order: PCI caps first, then extended caps

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Provides read/write access to device VPD for field service and asset tracking. Stores calibration, serials, and support data. Accessible via standardized VPD capability registers. Useful for manufacturing and inventory systems. Can be protected read‑only in production.
evidence: PF0_VPD_CAP (DSP) p. 1484–1486
dependencies: VPD area provisioned; permissions set
Remarks: Populate during manufacturing to avoid field gaps

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Defines a vendor‑specific capability that advertises the DMA engine and its unrolled register map. Provides base address offsets for channel blocks. Enables portable discovery by drivers rather than hard‑coded addresses. Integrates with PF capability space for enumeration. Improves compatibility across core revisions.
evidence: VSECDMA_EXT_CAP_HDR_OFF (USP) p. 3837; UNROLL_ADDR_OFFSET_LOW/HIGH p. 3845
dependencies: CC_DMA_ENABLE; capability present in PF space
Remarks: Use VSEC to discover channels instead of static tables

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Optional Compute Express Link transport DVSECs expose CXL‑specific control/status when licensed. Enables CXL.io interoperability and FlexBus structures. Coexists with PCIe capabilities in the same function. Requires platform CXL stack for meaningful use. Discovery via PCIe DVSEC headers.
evidence: CXL‑RCIEP Transport DVSEC (USP) p. 3920; CXL RCRB DVSEC (DSP) p. 2045–2049; (USP) p. 3878–3882
dependencies: CX_CXL_ENABLE; platform CXL software stack
Remarks: Only enable when all link partners support CXL

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
feature_name: MSI Reception Status and GPIO Debug
description: Provides internal MSI reception status and optional GPIO I/O for testing. Aids in validating interrupt delivery paths. Useful during bring‑up before OS drivers are stable. Enables quick visual or polled confirmation of vectors. Complements standard MSI capability programming.
evidence: INTR0 Status +0x830 (USP) p. 2243; MSI_GPIO_IO_OFF +0x888 p. 2244
dependencies: MSI enabled and routed; access to USP block
Remarks: Good for firmware‑level smoke tests of interrupt paths

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Built‑in event counters record protocol and traffic metrics. Time‑based analysis registers provide timestamped observations. Helps profile workloads and diagnose performance anomalies. Supports enable/clear/read flows for low overhead telemetry. Valuable for regression and field diagnostics.
evidence: EVENT_COUNTER_CONTROL/DATA p. 1784–1787; TIME_BASED_ANALYSIS_* p. 1788–1791; (USP) p. 3607–3614
dependencies: Counter block enabled; access serialized with traffic
Remarks: Sample at steady state; avoid perturbing workload

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Provides containment and recovery for downstream port fatal errors. Helps prevent error propagation by isolating faulty endpoints. Works with AER to signal events to root complex. Facilitates automated link reset and recovery policies. Improves system availability in the presence of device faults.
evidence: Signal Descriptions: DPC signals (p. 1203)
dependencies: Feature availability depends on device type/licensing
Remarks: Enable with caution; verify firmware recovery flow

controller_name: PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DesignWare PCIe DM)
description: Allows controlled writes to fields that are read‑only from standard PCIe perspective. Used to change strap defaults or board‑specific parameters. Must be enabled only transiently to avoid accidental corruption. Subject to core DBI access rules. Essential for low‑level board bring‑up adjustments.
evidence: Programming Notes: DBI write rules (Table 3‑768 ref); DBI_RO_WR_EN mention in notes
dependencies: DBI access path available; privileged firmware context
Remarks: Disable override immediately after making required changes
